title: senecio sarcoides
intro: Senecio sarcoides, also known as Raisin Bush, is a shrubby perennial plant native to New Zealand. It contains alkaloids such as senkirkine, senecionine, and seneciovidine, which have been found to have medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and emetic. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Senecio sarcoides are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to the potential toxicity of its alkaloids.
